name: Particle Cloud API
what_it_does: IoT platform API for managing Particle-powered cellular, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth devices with remote function calls, event publishing, and OTA firmware updates.
use_cases: Calling functions on remote IoT devices from agents
Reading real-time sensor data and variables from devices
Publishing and subscribing to IoT events in agent workflows
Triggering OTA firmware updates across device fleets
Monitoring device connectivity and fleet health
not_for: Non-Particle hardware (tightly coupled to Particle ecosystem)
Very large scale (10K+ device) deployments without enterprise plan
best_when: You need a full-stack IoT platform with cellular connectivity, reliable device management, and clean REST API for Particle hardware.
avoid_when: You need hardware-agnostic IoT, AWS/Azure-scale deployments, or sub-100ms real-time requirements.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for user tokens. Product bearer tokens for product-scoped access. Customer tokens for end-user access delegation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 100 devices, basic features
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth $19/mo (unlimited devices + cellular data), Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Cellular data plans for Particle SIM cards billed separately. Hardware purchase required. Very competitive pricing for IoT.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 API calls/5 minutes per access token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include error, error_description, and HTTP status. Well-documented with examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Function calls to devices are not idempotent. Duplicate calls execute the function multiple times.
known_agent_gotchas: Device function calls fail silently if device is offline - check device online status first
Rate limits are per token, not per device - aggregate rate limits can be hit in fleet scenarios
Events have a short TTL and may not be delivered if device was offline when published
Product access tokens vs user tokens have different scopes - product tokens recommended for agents
Webhook integrations require Particle-specific setup, not standard webhooks

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with device/user scopes. OAuth access tokens with TTLs. TLS for device communication. Device-level product keys. Particle's cloud manages device authentication.
